theme: grow a tree that outsmarts its own shade
title: Canopy Spacing: A Tree That Outgrows Its Own Shade
innovation_hook: Light harvest is capped by self-occlusion, so the win is geometric spacing of foliage rather than adding more of it.
strategies: trivial baseline: reproduce the checker's own modest fixed-angle reference canopy

textbook greedy: hard-code the classic ~22 degree fractal-tree branching angle with full leaf retention (taper=1), ignoring the instance's cost coefficients and sun schedule -- self-shades once the crown gets deep or the sun schedule is wide

adaptive local search: simulate the checker's own harvested-light-minus-biomass-cost objective locally and run a seeded coordinate/random search over (length ratio, branching angle, leaf taper, asymmetry) against the actual instance data
(headroom above strong): a smarter continuous optimizer / exchange argument tuned per sun-ray weight could still find better spacing than the local search's ~320 evaluations
